title: Expressing support for the designation of March 6, 2025, as "Great Lakes Day".
plain_summary: This bill asks Congress to make March 6, 2025 a special day called Great Lakes Day. The Great Lakes are five very large lakes that are important to America because they give drinking water to millions of people. These lakes also help businesses make money and give families places to have fun like swimming and boating. The bill says we need to keep protecting these lakes so they stay clean and healthy for everyone to use.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Water Resources and Environment.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution expresses support for making March sixth, two thousand twenty-five a special day called Great Lakes Day.

sections.title: Facts About the Great Lakes
sections.text: The Great Lakes are five large lakes called Lake Michigan, Lake Huron, Lake Erie, Lake Ontario, and Lake Superior. These lakes touch eight states: Michigan, Wisconsin, Minnesota, Ohio, Indiana, Illinois, Pennsylvania, and New York. The area around the Great Lakes is home to many types of wildlife including moose, Canada lynx, river otters, bald eagles, and many kinds of birds and fish. The Great Lakes have beautiful parks, forests, shores, and islands that people enjoy visiting for swimming, hiking, picnics, boating, fishing, hunting, and watching wildlife. Thirty-five million people live in the states around the Great Lakes today, and Native American tribes have called this area home for thousands of years. The Great Lakes make up the largest freshwater system on the surface of the Earth. They hold more than ninety percent of all the freshwater in the United States and more than twenty percent of all the freshwater in the world. More than forty million people get their drinking water from the Great Lakes. The Great Lakes are very important for business and the economy. Ships use them to move goods from place to place. The Great Lakes region adds three point one trillion dollars to the country's total economic output. The region provides jobs for more than twenty-five million people and pays out one point three trillion dollars in wages. The health of the Great Lakes is essential for both the environment and the economy of the region. The Great Lakes are an important part of America's history and heritage. Lake St. Clair is called the Heart of the Great Lakes because of where it sits and its size, and because it helps provide drinking water, recreation, and business opportunities.

sections.title: What the House of Representatives Resolves
sections.text: The House of Representatives supports making Great Lakes Day an official designation. The House recognizes how much the Great Lakes contribute to the way people live in the United States. The House promises to make sure the Great Lakes are preserved and protected through programs like the Great Lakes Restoration Initiative for people today and for future generations. The House calls on all Americans and all levels of government in the United States to take more action and speed up current efforts to preserve and protect Lake St. Clair as an important natural resource.
